preview_text:
The model favors Belgium at 38%, but the gap to Iran's 30% is modest. An Iran upset remains plausible under the statistical forecast, which assigns the underdog nearly one-in-three odds. This is a notably tight matchup in model space, with Belgium holding only a single-digit percentage-point edge.

The draw probability of 32% reflects competitive balance in the model's projection. The spread across outcomes—38%, 32%, 30%—suggests the match is unlikely to feel one-sided. All three results carry meaningful weight, and the relatively high draw chance indicates the teams are expected to converge on a competitive equilibrium.

The sportsbook and Polymarket consensus diverge sharply from the model. Both sources elevate Belgium to roughly 68–70% and compress Iran to 10–11%, a spread roughly 29 percentage points wider than the model estimate. The market assigns the draw 20% or less, versus the model's 31%. This represents a material gap; the dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment better reflects true match probability.
